description: Fix Add to Calendar timezone bug — store user's IANA timezone on profile, auto-detect from browser, and generate .ics files with VTIMEZONE so events appear at the intended local time instead of UTC

userStories.title: Add timezone field to UserProfile shared type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store a user's IANA timezone string on their profile so it can be used when generating calendar events.
acceptanceCriteria: Add optional `timezone?: string` field to the `UserProfile` interface in `packages/shared/src/types.ts`
Field stores IANA timezone identifiers (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London', 'Asia/Tokyo')
Rebuild shared package with `cd packages/shared && yarn build`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Accept and persist timezone on profile creation and update
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the API to accept a timezone field when creating or updating a user profile so the frontend can send the detected timezone.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /users` in `services/api/src/routes/users.ts` accepts optional `timezone` field in request body and stores it on the profile (add to destructuring around line 121 and to the profile object around line 139)

`PUT /users/me` in `services/api/src/routes/users.ts` accepts optional `timezone` field and includes it in the updates object (add to destructuring around line 426 and to updates logic around line 437)
Invalid timezone values (empty string, non-string) are silently ignored — do not reject the request
Existing profile data is not affected when timezone is not provided in the request body
Add unit tests in `services/api/src/routes/users.test.ts`: profile creation with timezone, profile update with timezone, profile update without timezone (no regression)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Generate timezone-aware .ics files with VTIMEZONE
userStories.description: As a user, I want the downloaded .ics file to contain events in my local timezone so they appear at the time I intended on my calendar.
acceptanceCriteria: `generateIcs()` in `services/api/src/lib/ics-generator.ts` accepts an optional `timezone` parameter (IANA string, e.g., 'America/New_York')
When timezone is provided, the .ics file includes a minimal VTIMEZONE component with TZID and X-LIC-LOCATION set to the IANA timezone
When timezone is provided, DTSTART and DTEND use `TZID=<timezone>:YYYYMMDDTHHmmss` format (no Z suffix) instead of UTC Z suffix
When timezone is NOT provided (legacy/fallback), behavior is unchanged — events use UTC with Z suffix as before
Add a `formatDateTimeLocal()` helper that produces `YYYYMMDDTHHmmss` (no Z suffix) for timezone-aware events
The schedule time '07:00' with timezone 'America/New_York' results in DTSTART;TZID=America/New_York:...T070000 (7:00 AM Eastern, not UTC)

Add unit tests in a new or existing test file: ICS generation with timezone produces VTIMEZONE block and TZID parameters, ICS generation without timezone still produces UTC Z-suffix times, different timezone values produce correct VTIMEZONE blocks
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pass user timezone from profile to calendar endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the burn buddy and burn squad calendar endpoints to look up the requesting user's timezone and pass it to the ICS generator.
acceptanceCriteria: `GET /burn-buddies/:id/calendar` in `services/api/src/routes/burn-buddies.ts` (around line 393) reads the requesting user's profile from Firestore to get their `timezone` field, and passes it to `generateIcs()` as the timezone option

`GET /burn-squads/:id/calendar` in `services/api/src/routes/burn-squads.ts` reads the requesting user's profile from Firestore to get their `timezone` field, and passes it to `generateIcs()` as the timezone option
If the user has no timezone stored (field is undefined), the endpoints omit the timezone parameter so generateIcs() falls back to UTC (current behavior)
No additional API calls are needed from the client — timezone comes from the server-side profile lookup
Add unit tests: calendar download with user timezone set produces VTIMEZONE in response, calendar download without timezone falls back to UTC format
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auto-detect and send timezone from web app on session start
userStories.description: As a user, I want my timezone to be automatically detected and saved so my calendar exports show the correct time — without needing to configure anything.
acceptanceCriteria: On login or session restore (after Firebase Auth state is confirmed), the web app reads the browser's timezone via `Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone`

The detected timezone is sent to `PUT /users/me` with the `timezone` field — this can be added to the existing auth state handler in the web app (look in `apps/web/src/` for the auth context or provider that calls the API on login)
This happens silently on every session start (login or page load with existing session), keeping the timezone current if the user travels
If `Intl.DateTimeFormat` is unavailable or throws, the timezone update is skipped gracefully — no errors shown to the user, no console errors
The timezone update must not block or delay the user's login flow — it runs as a fire-and-forget side effect
Typecheck passes
